flow.label: Verify your qualifications
flow.note: Primary source verification through the AMC (EPIC).

flow.label: AMC clinical exam, or a workplace-based assessment (WBA)
flow.note: Two routes to prove clinical competence.

flow.label: Supervised practice
flow.note: In an approved position with a supervisor.

flow.label: General registration
flow.note: Practise as a doctor in Australia.

title: The Standard pathway
metaTitle: Standard pathway for IMGs in Australia: AMC exams, steps & timeline
metaDescription: The Standard pathway for International Medical Graduates in Australia: the AMC CAT MCQ exam, the AMC clinical exam or workplace-based assessment, English requirements and how it leads to general registration.
summaryMarkdown: The Standard pathway is the route for International Medical Graduates (IMGs) who aren't eligible for the competent authority or specialist pathways. [[cite:std-who]] You demonstrate your knowledge and clinical skills through the Australian Medical Council's (AMC) assessments, which are pitched at the level of an Australian medical graduate about to start internship. [[cite:std-level]] It leads to general registration with the Medical Board of Australia.

sections.heading: Who the Standard pathway is for
sections.bodyMarkdown:
You'll generally use the Standard pathway if your primary medical qualification isn't from a country Australia recognises as a *competent authority*, and you aren't applying as a specialist. [[cite:std-who]]

If you trained or are certified in the UK, Ireland, USA, Canada or New Zealand, check the [Competent Authority pathway](/pathways/competent-authority) first, it usually skips the AMC exams. If you hold an overseas specialist qualification, see the [Specialist](/pathways/specialist) and [Expedited Specialist](/pathways/expedited-specialist) pathways.

sections.heading: The AMC exams
sections.bodyMarkdown:
The Standard pathway has two assessment stages, both run by the AMC: [[cite:std-exams]]

1. **AMC CAT MCQ examination**, a computer-adaptive, multiple-choice exam testing medical knowledge.
2. **AMC Clinical examination *or* a Workplace-Based Assessment (WBA)**, testing clinical skills. The WBA is an AMC-accredited alternative completed in an approved workplace under supervision.

Both are set at the standard of a newly qualified Australian medical graduate about to begin intern training. [[cite:std-level]] Before sitting, you create an AMC portfolio and have your qualifications verified through primary source verification. [[cite:std-psv]]

sections.heading: English language requirement
sections.bodyMarkdown: You must meet the Medical Board's English language skills registration standard. Accepted tests include IELTS Academic, OET, PTE Academic and TOEFL iBT, and results are generally valid for two years. [[cite:std-english]] See [English requirements](/english) for the detail.

sections.heading: From exams to registration
sections.bodyMarkdown: Passing the AMC assessments makes you eligible to apply to the Medical Board for registration. In practice you usually hold provisional registration while you complete a period of supervised practice (similar to an internship), then move to general registration. [[cite:std-registration]] Registration types are explained on the [registration](/registration) page.

sections.heading: Visas and where you can work
sections.bodyMarkdown: Most IMGs on this pathway work in employer-sponsored roles, commonly on the Skills in Demand (subclass 482) visa. [[cite:std-visa]] As an IMG you may also be subject to the Medicare *10-year moratorium*, which affects where you can bill Medicare, see [Medicare & the moratorium](/medicare) and [where you can work](/where-you-can-work). Migration is regulated: confirm your situation with a registered MARA agent.

claims.text: The Standard pathway is for IMGs not eligible for the competent authority or specialist assessment pathways.
AMC examinations and assessment are set at the level of a newly qualified Australian medical graduate about to begin intern training.
The Standard pathway requires the AMC CAT MCQ examination plus either the AMC Clinical examination or a Workplace-Based Assessment.
Applicants create an AMC portfolio and have qualifications verified via primary source verification.
Accepted English tests include IELTS Academic, OET, PTE Academic and TOEFL iBT, with results generally valid for two years.
IMGs typically hold provisional registration during supervised practice before moving to general registration.
The Skills in Demand (subclass 482) visa is the main employer-sponsored work visa used for doctors.

faq.question: Do I have to sit both the MCQ and the clinical exam?
faq.answerMarkdown: You sit the AMC CAT MCQ exam, then complete **either** the AMC Clinical examination **or** an AMC-accredited Workplace-Based Assessment, not both.

faq.question: How long does the Standard pathway take?
faq.answerMarkdown: It varies widely depending on exam availability, when you secure a supervised post and processing times. Treat any single figure with caution and plan around your own exam dates. See [costs & timelines](/costs-and-timelines).

faq.question: Is the Standard pathway harder than the others?
faq.answerMarkdown: It involves more assessment than the competent authority pathway because Australia hasn't pre-recognised your training system. It's not a judgement of your ability, it's about how your qualification is verified.
